Politics.headline: Iran talks to resume this weekend without Vance
Politics.detail: The White House will send Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, as Tehran decides not to send its top negotiator.

Politics.headline: Trump administration moves to add firing squad to federal execution methods
Politics.detail: The Trump administration is moving to add firing squad as an option for federal executions.

Politics.headline: Trump orders more access to psychedelics like LSD, psilocybin and ibogaine
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on researching the effects of psychedelic drugs in medical.

Politics.headline: Appeals court blocks Trump's asylum crackdown at U.S.-Mexico border
Politics.detail: A three-judge panel from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit found that immigration laws give people the right to apply for asylum at the border, and the president can't circumvent that.

Politics.headline: DOJ drops criminal probe into Fed chair Jerome Powell
Politics.detail: The Justice Department dropped its probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell after admitting it lacked evidence.

Finance & Economy.headline: Stocks mostly rise on peace hopes and tech outperformance
Finance & Economy.detail: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit fresh record highs after encouraging signs that the U.S. and Iran may return to the negotiating table for peace talks; technology stocks led the gains, with semiconductors outperforming after Intel issued an upbeat outlook.

Finance & Economy.headline: Intel’s best day since 1987 leads the US stock market to more records
Finance & Economy.detail: A surge for Intel following a blowout profit report led the U.S. stock market to more records, while oil prices kept yo-yoing in the wait for what's next with the Iran war.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil rises above $106 per barrel as US, Iran deadlocked in Strait of Hormuz
Finance & Economy.detail: The jump in prices comes as Donald Trump says vessels will need permission of US Navy to transit key waterway.

Finance & Economy.headline: China's DeepSeek unveils latest models a year after upending global tech
Finance & Economy.detail: DeepSeek, a Chinese AI firm, has released its latest models, marking a year since it disrupted the global technology landscape.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed chair Jerome Powell
Finance & Economy.detail: The US Justice Department has dropped its criminal probe of Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell.

Technology.headline: Ghana vows to accelerate AI development
Technology.detail: Ghanaian President John Dramani Mahama launched the country's National Artificial Intelligence (AI) Strategy, pledging 270 million U.S. dollars to accelerate the development of the sector and position Ghana as a leading digital innovation hub in Africa. The investment will be directed towards critical infrastructure and funding the implementation of the strategy, with 300,000 Ghanaians to be trained under the One Million Coders Program to build digital and AI skills.

Technology.headline: CSIST to partner with Saronic Tech on USVs
Technology.detail: The Chungshan Institute of Science and Technology (CSIST) has signed an MOU with Saronic Technologies to jointly develop modular uncrewed surface vehicles (USVs), enhancing Taiwan's AI control systems for USVs. The collaboration aims to reduce development time and diversify high-end technology sources, ensuring rapid USV production in emergencies.

Technology.headline: Technology allows govt to anticipate dangerous weather
Technology.detail: The Malaysian government is utilizing AI to anticipate dangerous weather conditions, enhancing data collection and management for faster disaster response. Predictive AI is being used to forecast heavy rainfall, improving early warning systems and enabling more informed action by responders.

Technology.headline: DeepSeek previews new model adapted for Huawei chips
Technology.detail: DeepSeek, a Chinese AI startup, has launched a preview of its new V4 model adapted for Huawei chip technology, highlighting China's growing capabilities in the AI sector. The model outperforms other open-source models in world-knowledge benchmarks and marks a shift from DeepSeek's reliance on Nvidia chips.

Technology.headline: US flags DeepSeek in global warning on Chinese AI distillation
Technology.detail: The US State Department has directed diplomats worldwide to raise concerns about Chinese firms, including DeepSeek, allegedly distilling US AI models, escalating the technology dispute with Beijing. The warning aims to highlight the risks of using AI models derived from US proprietary technology and prepare for potential government follow-up.

Science.headline: US seeking to halt AI model 'exploitation'
Science.detail: The US government is planning measures to prevent foreign entities, particularly those in China, from improperly using US AI models to build competing chatbots by extracting proprietary information.

Science.headline: Scientists Identify Protein Behind Ageing Joints In Arthritis Breakthrough
Science.detail: Stanford University scientists have identified a protein called 15-PGDH that drives cartilage breakdown with age, and shown that blocking it can restore worn joints in mice and human tissue.

Science.headline: NASA Scientist Says a "Fifth Force" May Be Hiding in Our Solar System
Science.detail: Scientists are investigating why the universe behaves differently on massive scales compared to our solar system, suggesting a possible undiscovered "fifth force".

Science.headline: Moon to occult the bright star Regulus
Science.detail: On April 25/26, 2026, the Moon will occult the bright star Regulus and most of the US will be treated to this occultation of the bright star Regulus by the Moon.

Science.headline: Power Of "and" In Energy And Climate Entrepreneurship
Science.detail: Georgina Campbell Flatter, the CEO of Greentown Labs, spoke of the importance of multiple people, companies, and solutions collaborating to advance energy and climate solutions.

Health.headline: World Malaria Day 2026 - Driven to end malaria: Now we can. Now we must
Health.detail: The World Health Organization is calling for renewed commitments to build more sustainable national programmes, stronger integration with primary healthcare, and more prioritisation on the part of global health agencies and partners to end malaria.

Health.headline: Mount Sinai Health System and Guyana Ministry of Health Host Seminar on Practical, Responsible Integration of Artificial Intelligence in Health Care
Health.detail: The Mount Sinai Health System and the Guyana Ministry of Health convened leaders to discuss the integration of AI into healthcare, focusing on practical foundations, ethical considerations, and real-world decision-making frameworks to support safe and effective adoption.

Health.headline: WHO-WMO Accelerator Hits New Climate-Health Milestones
Health.detail: The WHO-WMO Climate and Health Science and Services Accelerator is scaling up the use of climate services for health, marked by the launch of the South Asia Climate-Health Desk and the Africa Climate-Health Desk, along with the creation of the South Asia Hub of the Global Heat Health Information Network.

Health.headline: Only four European countries meet flu vaccination target: WHO Europe
Health.detail: Only four countries in the WHO European region have met a two-decade-old global target for influenza vaccination among older adults in the 2022-2023 season, with inequalities in vaccine access and uptake remaining a concern. The analysis showed a tenfold gap in dose availability within the region, with high-income countries distributing significantly more doses per person than lower-middle-income countries.

Health.headline: Today's bloated NDIS would never have been greenlit, its former head says. How did we get here?
Health.detail: An ex-head of the NDIS (National Disability Insurance Scheme) discusses the scheme's current state.

Sports.headline: Virat Kohli inspires Royal Challengers Bengaluru to 5-wicket win
Sports.detail: Virat Kohli and Devdutt Padikkal's blazing 115-run stand set up RCB's victory as Sai Sudharsan's century goes in vain, helping Royal Challengers Bengaluru move to 2nd in the IPL standings.

Sports.headline: Thomas And Uber Cup Finals: Indian Men Beat Canada 4-1; Women Lose To Denmark
Sports.detail: The Indian men's badminton team secured a 4-1 victory over Canada in the Thomas and Uber Cup Finals, while the women's team lost to Denmark.

Sports.headline: New rule now lets players represent both New Zealand and an Origin side
Sports.detail: A new rule has been introduced that allows players to represent both New Zealand and an Origin side in Rugby.

Sports.headline: Rangers Pull Away Late to Take Series Opener
Sports.detail: Josh Doelitsch pitched a solid 6.0 innings as Northwestern Oklahoma State took the series opener against SWOSU in baseball, with SWOSU needing to win a Saturday doubleheader to secure a postseason berth.

Sports.headline: Ponga shuns rival clubs, codes to ink mega extension
Sports.detail: Kalyn Ponga has signed a mega extension with the Knights, ensuring he will remain with the team for at least 13 seasons since his debut in 2018.

Entertainment.headline: 'Landman' star Ali Larter shows off her toned abs in daring bralette and cargo pants look
Entertainment.detail: Ali Larter recently appeared showcasing a bralette and cargo pants outfit.

Entertainment.headline: Jake Reiner describes the 'living nightmare' of losing his parents, Rob and Michele Reiner
Entertainment.detail: Jake Reiner, the older son of Rob and Michele Reiner, described the devastating impact of learning about his parents' death.

Entertainment.headline: Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle dies at 92
Entertainment.detail: The famous Bollywood singer, Asha Bhosle, has passed away at the age of 92.

Entertainment.headline: Dan Walker's former co-host withdraws 'bullying' claims as agreement reached
Entertainment.detail: Claudia–Liza Vanderpuije has withdrawn claims against her former Channel 5 News co-host Dan Walker after reaching a “mutual agreement” with the company.

Entertainment.headline: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its impressive box office run, nearing Rs 1,170 crore worldwide and Rs 1,125 crore in India
Entertainment.detail: Despite facing competition from new releases like Hollywood's 'Michael' and Bollywood's 'Bhoot Bangla', the spy drama added Rs 1.60 crore on its 37th day, showcasing its sustained popularity.

Crime & Justice.headline: US Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department will adopt firing squads as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases, and is also reauthorizing the use of single-drug lethal injections with pentobarbital.

Crime & Justice.headline: Olabi says arrest of criminal Amjad Youssef a key step towards justice
Crime & Justice.detail: Syria's permanent representative to the United Nations, Ibrahim Olabi, affirmed that the arrest of Amjad Youssef constitutes an important milestone in the path of transitional justice, stressing that this process will continue until all those responsible for crimes committed against the Syrian people are apprehended. The crime was committed in the Tadamon neighborhood, where victims were killed and buried in a mass grave.

Crime & Justice.headline: US sanctions Cambodian senator over purported scam network links
Crime & Justice.detail: The US Treasury Department accuses Kok An and associates of using political influence to protect network of fraud centres.

Crime & Justice.headline: Arrest made in case of missing USF doctoral students, 1 found dead
Crime & Justice.detail: Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister said Zamil Limon's remains were found on the Howard Frankland bridge Friday morning, but Nahida Bristy is still missing.

Crime & Justice.headline: Federal Jury Convicts Registered Sex Offender of Sexually Exploiting Two Minors and Obstructing Justice
Crime & Justice.detail: A federal jury has convicted a registered sex offender of sexually exploiting two minors and obstructing justice.

Education.headline: CBSE 10th 2nd Board Exams 2026: 6.68 Lakh Enrolments Recorded, Science Most Preferred
Education.detail: The CBSE (Central Board of Secondary Education) recorded 6.68 lakh enrollments for the 10th-grade second board exams in 2026, with science being the most preferred subject. These exams provide students with an opportunity to improve their scores.

Education.headline: No Leaves For Medical College Students On May 2, 3 Ahead Of NEET UG 2026: Says NMC
Education.detail: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has announced that medical college students will not be granted leave on May 2nd and 3rd, ahead of the NEET UG 2026 exam. This decision ensures that students are available for any exam-related duties or preparations.

Education.headline: Sweden Cuts Screen Use In Classrooms To Tackle Falling Literacy Rates
Education.detail: Sweden is reducing the use of screens in classrooms and reintroducing books to combat declining literacy rates. This move aims to encourage traditional reading and writing skills among students.

Education.headline: As Delhi Braces For Heatwave, Schools Asked To Act, Report
Education.detail: In anticipation of a heatwave, schools in Delhi have been instructed to take necessary precautions and report their actions. These measures are to protect students from the extreme heat.

Education.headline: Schools forced to cut back on support for Send pupils in England, poll finds
Education.detail: A recent poll reveals that schools in England are being compelled to diminish the level of assistance provided to students with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) due to financial constraints. This reduction in support raises concerns about the well-being and academic progress of vulnerable students.

Lifestyle.headline: Viral Video: Meghalaya Wedding Sees Bride And Groom Vroom In On Bikes
Lifestyle.detail: A wedding in Meghalaya went viral after the bride and groom made a grand entrance on motorcycles.

Lifestyle.headline: Inside Restaurant Week India 2026: How to book, where to go, and what it says about dining today
Lifestyle.detail: Restaurant Week India 2026 offers a look into current dining trends, providing information on how to book and where to find participating restaurants.

Lifestyle.headline: Women reveal surprising effects of being bald: 'It's a luxury'
Lifestyle.detail: Women discuss the unexpected benefits and experiences of choosing to be bald.

Lifestyle.headline: 'It is impossible to even begin imagining': Why 83-year-old Amitabh Bachchan's 'work' mantra is a masterclass in ageing well.
Lifestyle.detail: An examination of Amitabh Bachchan's approach to work at 83 years old and the lessons it offers on aging well.

Lifestyle.headline: Leggings are back – now with added polish
Lifestyle.detail: An article discussing the resurgence of leggings in the fashion world, but now with a more refined and stylish approach.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: How Asia should deal with the 'Malacca dilemma'
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The militarization of the Strait of Hormuz has raised concerns about the vulnerability of the Strait of Malacca, a crucial shipping route, to geopolitical shifts and potential disruptions, especially amidst US-China rivalry. Southeast Asia's divisions could complicate crisis containment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: I am still becoming
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece reflects on the author's journey of education and personal growth, emphasizing perseverance and resilience in overcoming challenges. It highlights the importance of continuous learning and viewing setbacks as opportunities for growth.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Mark Kelly's 180 days of socialism
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post's Editorial Board criticizes the idea that government reports and a president-directed economy will bring prices down. The editorial expresses skepticism about socialist approaches to economic problems.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — April 25, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article likely contains various letters to the editor on different topics. One letter discusses judicial independence and the resignation of judges, arguing against it being used as an escape route for those facing impeachment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Asia's next harvest is already being decided
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article discusses factors influencing Asia's upcoming harvest. It touches upon topics such as climate resilience and social protection in the region.

World.headline: Witkoff, Kushner heading to Pakistan for new Iran peace talks
World.detail: U.S. Envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will head to Pakistan on Saturday morning (April 25, 2026) for a new round of talks with Iran on ending the war, the White House said. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed the trip, stating they would engage in talks with representatives from the Iranian delegation.

World.headline: Iran and U.S. dispatch negotiators to Pakistan for new talks after delays
World.detail: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi's trip comes as much of the world has been on edge over a war that has snarled crucial energy exports through the Strait of Hormuz. The talks are aimed at de-escalating tensions and finding a path towards a peaceful resolution.

World.headline: Tehran's Imam Khomeini Airport airport to resume international flights on April 25
World.detail: International flights from Tehran's Imam Khomeini Airport will resume on Saturday (April 25, 2026), days after Iran reopened its airspace. The first flights to resume will be to Istanbul and Muscat, the announcement said.

World.headline: Netanyahu says he was treated for early-stage prostate cancer
World.detail: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says the successful operation for prostate cancer happened 18 months ago and that he is now in 'excellent physical condition'. He stated that he hid the diagnosis from the public because of the ongoing war.

World.headline: China slaps export curbs on European firms over Taiwan arms sales
World.detail: China has imposed export restrictions on several European companies due to their involvement in arms sales to Taiwan. This action is likely to further escalate tensions between China and several European nations.

Zimbabwe.headline: Minister Polite Kambamura Cracks Down on Proxy Mining Deals, Warns of Imminent Policy Shift
Zimbabwe.detail: Minister Polite Kambamura is taking a firm stance against proxy mining deals and has warned of a coming policy shift. This move signals a government intention to regulate the mining sector more strictly and combat illegal activities.

Zimbabwe.headline: Manica Diamonds Seek Approval to Disband First Team
Zimbabwe.detail: Manica Diamonds FC, a local football club, is seeking ZIFA approval to disband its senior team. The reasons behind this decision are currently unknown.

Zimbabwe.headline: Farmers Union warns rising costs threaten winter wheat season production
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Farmers Union (ZFU) has cautioned that escalating costs, particularly for fuel, are jeopardizing the upcoming winter wheat season. The current producer price structure is misaligned with the realities on the ground, leaving farmers exposed to significant financial strain.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chatunga Mugabe and co-accused paid shooting victim R250,000 cash to silence him – court hears
Zimbabwe.detail: Chatunga Mugabe, son of the late former president, and his co-accused allegedly paid R250,000 to a shooting victim to silence him, according to court proceedings. The details surrounding the case continue to unfold.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mwonzora drags police to court over ban on anti–Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 protest
Zimbabwe.detail: Mwonzora is challenging the police's decision to ban a protest against Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 in court. The legal action highlights the ongoing dispute over the proposed constitutional changes.
